title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer flags one blocking issue: the WITH_PROMETHEUS CI path runs `helm install` without `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true`, so no ServiceMonitor is rendered and chainsaw steps 2–3 fail. They want `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh` (and the `make deploy-infra` path) updated to pass that flag when `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true`, preferably by threading the env var into the helm invocation.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review c5c4853 with Claude Code

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=1 critical=1 warning=10 info=3 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## BLOCKING (1)

### B-001: WITH_PROMETHEUS path does not produce ServiceMonitor in CI flow
**File**: `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh:50-55` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: In the CI e2e-prometheus job, hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh runs `helm install` without passing `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true`. With chart default `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled: false`, the gated template renders zero output — no ServiceMonitor in `openstack`. The kind/base kustomization suspends HelmRelease/keystone-operator so the patch in `enable_keystone_operator_servicemonitor` is durable but inert. As a result, chainsaw step 2 (servicemonitor-exists) fails and step 3 (prometheus-target-up) cascades; the e2e-prometheus job has continue-on-error: false, so this is a hard fail. Cascades to REQ-005, REQ-003 and REQ-009 step 2/3 assertions. The same defect exists in the local `make deploy-infra` path because the suspend patch is in kind/base.

**Code**:
```
helm install "${OPERATOR}-operator" "${CHART_PATH}/" \
  --set "image.repository=${IMAGE_REPO}" \
  --set "image.tag=${IMAGE_TAG}" \
  --set image.pullPolicy=Never \
  --wait --timeout 120s
```

**Action Required**: Either (a) thread WITH_PROMETHEUS into hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh and append `--set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` when set; (b) export WITH_PROMETHEUS to the operator chart values via a `--values` file emitted by deploy-infra.sh; or (c) drop the kind/base suspend on keystone-operator for the prometheus path so Flux can apply the patch. Option (a) is the smallest change.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Thread WITH_PROMETHEUS into hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh and append `--set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` to the helm install invocation when WITH_PROMETHEUS=true. Update the e2e-prometheus job in .github/workflows/ci.yaml to export WITH_PROMETHEUS=true before invoking ci-deploy-operator.sh.
```

**Related**: W-001, W-005, W-007

---

## CRITICAL (1)

### C-001: ci-deploy-operator.sh ignores patched ServiceMonitor value, e2e-prometheus will fail
**File**: `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh:49-56` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml patch suspends the keystone-operator Flux HelmRelease so CI can `helm install` the chart with a locally built dev image. In the new e2e-prometheus lane, `make deploy-infra` (with WITH_PROMETHEUS=true) calls `enable_keystone_operator_servicemonitor` which patches the suspended HelmRelease's spec.values — but because it is suspended, Flux never reconciles, so the patch is inert. The next CI step `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh` does not read the HelmRelease's spec.values and does not pass `--set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true`. The chart default is `enabled: false`, so the templates/servicemonitor.yaml guard renders nothing. Chainsaw Step 2 `servicemonitor-exists` then fails. The PR's own log message and planning doc RISK-7/PITFALLS flagged this exact gap; it was never closed.

**Code**:
```
helm install "${OPERATOR}-operator" \
  "${CHART_PATH}/" \
  --set "image.repository=${IMAGE_REPO}" \
  --set "image.tag=${IMAGE_TAG}" \
  --set image.pullPolicy=Never \
  --wait --timeout 120s
```

**Action Required**: Wire WITH_PROMETHEUS through to ci-deploy-operator.sh and gate `--set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` on it, plus add WITH_PROMETHEUS: "true" to the e2e-prometheus job's Deploy operator step env. Alternatively, read spec.values.monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled from the HelmRelease and pass via --values so the deploy-infra patch becomes the single source of truth.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Wire WITH_PROMETHEUS through ci-deploy-operator.sh:

WITH_PROMETHEUS="${WITH_PROMETHEUS:-false}"

helm_args=(
  --set "image.repository=${IMAGE_REPO}"
  --set "image.tag=${IMAGE_TAG}"
  --set image.pullPolicy=Never
)
if [[ "${WITH_PROMETHEUS}" == "true" ]]; then
  helm_args+=(--set "monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true")
fi

helm install "${OPERATOR}-operator" "${CHART_PATH}/" "${helm_args[@]}" --wait --timeout 120s

Then add WITH_PROMETHEUS: "true" to the e2e-prometheus job's Deploy operator step env.
```

**Related**: W-001

---

## WARNING (10)

### W-001: Stale "Reviewer: please verify" comment in deploy-infra.sh
**File**: `hack/deploy-infra.sh:626` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Resolve ambiguous commented-out alternatives before merge

**Problem**: The DECISION comment block ends with `# Reviewer: please verify this matches intent (CC-0100, REQ-005).`. The project's own review pattern flags these as merge blockers, and the prior sourcery example explicitly removed a similar `Reviewer: please verify.` from buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy. Worse, the open question this comment raised about the suspend path was not actually answered (see Finding 1).

**Code**:
```
# Reviewer: please verify this matches intent (CC-0100, REQ-005).
```

**Action Required**: Either close the open question by fixing Finding 1 and rewriting the comment as stable rationale, or drop the leftover review prompt line.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
# DECISION: handle suspended HelmRelease in kind
# Ambiguity: deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml suspends the keystone-operator
#   HelmRelease (lines 87-106) so CI can `helm install` it manually with a
#   locally built image. A suspended HelmRelease never reconciles, so a
#   wait-for-Ready against it would always time out.
# Chose: patch spec.values regardless of suspend state, then read the current
#   spec.suspend; skip the wait when suspended (logging the rationale) and
#   wait for Ready otherwise. The patch remains durable so a future
#   un-suspend reconciles on the new values.
# Reason: matches the task contract literally for non-kind callers while
#   keeping the kind path green; the suspend semantics are owned by Flux, not
#   by this script.
```

**Related**: C-001

---

### W-002: kustomize build of deploy/kind/prometheus/ fails outside make deploy-infra
**File**: `deploy/kind/prometheus/kustomization.yaml:80-83` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The configMapGenerator references deploy/kind/prometheus/keystone-operator.json, but that file is gitignored and only written at deploy time by hack/deploy-infra.sh's `cp -f`. Any reasonable local-validation flow — `kustomize build deploy/kind/prometheus/`, `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/prometheus/`, or chainsaw lint of an inflated overlay — fails with `open .../keystone-operator.json: no such file or directory`. There is no documented prerequisite pointing readers at the canonical source path or the cp step.

**Code**:
```
configMapGenerator:
  - name: keystone-operator-dashboard
    namespace: monitoring
    files:
      - keystone-operator.json
```

**Action Required**: Choose one: (a) Add a Makefile helper `stage-prometheus-dashboard` that performs the copy and document it in infrastructure-manifests.md; (b) use kustomize CLI with LoadRestrictionsNone to point configMapGenerator at the canonical source via relative path; or (c) document in infrastructure-manifests.md that `kustomize build deploy/kind/prometheus/` requires running `make stage-prometheus-dashboard` (or hand-copying the JSON) first.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a Makefile helper:

.PHONY: stage-prometheus-dashboard
stage-prometheus-dashboard:
	cp -f operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json deploy/kind/prometheus/keystone-operator.json

And document the prerequisite in docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md under the Dashboard provisioning subsection. Alternatively, document the trade-off and the copy requirement directly so contributors know the local-validation flow.
```

---

### W-003: ci-workflow.md not updated for new e2e-prometheus and shell-unit-tests-cc-0100 jobs
**File**: `docs/reference/ci-cd/ci-workflow.md:87-127` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: ci-workflow.md:87 still declares "22 jobs" and :125-127 says "The four E2E jobs (e2e-infra, e2e-operator, e2e-chaos, tempest) share infrastructure setup ...". This PR adds two new jobs (e2e-prometheus, shell-unit-tests-cc-0100) bringing the count to 24 / five E2E jobs, with no corresponding per-job subsection (sibling sections for e2e-infra, e2e-operator, e2e-chaos exist at :464, :515, :550). The new infrastructure-manifests.md:809 cross-link `[CI / e2e-prometheus job](../ci-cd/)` resolves to a directory whose top-level ci-workflow.md has no entry for the linked job.

**Code**:
```
# ci-workflow.md:87, :125-127
... 22 jobs ...
The four E2E jobs (`e2e-infra`, `e2e-operator`, `e2e-chaos`, `tempest`) share infrastructure setup ...
```

**Action Required**: Update ci-workflow.md: bump job count to 24 at :87; add e2e-prometheus to the E2E Jobs DAG block (:110-114) with its needs: chain; update the "four E2E jobs" sentence (:125-126) to "five E2E jobs (e2e-infra, e2e-operator, e2e-chaos, e2e-prometheus, tempest)"; add an `### e2e-prometheus` subsection alongside `### e2e-chaos` at :550 describing WITH_PROMETHEUS env wiring, enable_keystone_operator_servicemonitor post-deploy step, and chainsaw gate semantics; add `### shell-unit-tests-cc-0100` near existing chainsaw-lint / verify-invalid-cr-fixtures entries.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Update job count from 22 to 24 at line 87; replace "four E2E jobs (e2e-infra, e2e-operator, e2e-chaos, tempest)" with "five E2E jobs (e2e-infra, e2e-operator, e2e-chaos, e2e-prometheus, tempest)" at lines 125-126; add e2e-prometheus to the DAG block at lines 110-114; add new `### e2e-prometheus` and `### shell-unit-tests-cc-0100` subsections following the existing per-job pattern.
```

### W-001: Missing planned test: step-1-assert-helmrelease-values-rendered (REQ-002)
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:95-150` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: Step 1 (helmrelease-ready) only asserts `kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready helmrelease/kube-prometheus-stack`. It does not query `.spec.values` to verify the four feature flags or `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention=6h`. The spec planned test `step-1-assert-helmrelease-values-rendered` is absent.

**Code**:
```
step 1 (helmrelease-ready): only asserts `kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready helmrelease/kube-prometheus-stack`
```

**Action Required**: Add a chainsaw assertion to step 1 that queries `kubectl get helmrelease kube-prometheus-stack -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.spec.values}'` and verifies `crds.enabled`, `alertmanager.enabled`, `nodeExporter.enabled`, `kubeStateMetrics.enabled` are false and `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention == "6h"`.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a chainsaw script step within step 1 that runs: kubectl get helmrelease kube-prometheus-stack -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.spec.values}' | jq -e '.crds.enabled == false and .alertmanager.enabled == false and .nodeExporter.enabled == false and .kubeStateMetrics.enabled == false and .prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention == "6h"'
```

**Related**: W-002, W-003

---

### W-002: Missing planned test: step-1-assert-no-alertmanager-no-node-exporter-no-kube-state-metrics (REQ-002)
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:95-150` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: No assertion in chainsaw-test.yaml checks for absence of Alertmanager StatefulSet, kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-node-exporter DaemonSet, or kube-prometheus-stack-kube-state-metrics Deployment in the monitoring namespace. The spec listed this as a separate planned test under REQ-002 scenario 1.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml has no step asserting absence of Alertmanager/node-exporter/kube-state-metrics workloads
```

**Action Required**: Add a chainsaw error step or a script that runs `kubectl get daemonset,statefulset,deployment -n monitoring` and asserts none of `kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-node-exporter-*`, `kube-prometheus-stack-kube-state-metrics-*`, or Alertmanager StatefulSet appear.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a chainsaw script step that runs: kubectl get daemonset,statefulset,deployment -n monitoring -o name | grep -E '(kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-node-exporter|kube-prometheus-stack-kube-state-metrics|alertmanager)' && exit 1 || exit 0
```

**Related**: W-001, W-003

### W-003: Missing planned test: step-1-assert-no-crd-flap-on-servicemonitors (REQ-002)
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:95-150` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: The chainsaw suite never inspects the servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com CRD's helm-managed-by annotation. The dual-owner reconcile flap that REQ-002 scenario 2 explicitly calls out as a non-goal is therefore unmonitored.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml has no inspection of servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com CRD ownership annotation
```

**Action Required**: Add a chainsaw step that queries `kubectl get crd servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com -o jsonpath='{.metadata.annotations.meta\.helm\.sh/release-name}'` and asserts the value equals `prometheus-operator-crds`.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a chainsaw script step that runs: test "$(kubectl get crd servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com -o jsonpath='{.metadata.annotations.meta\.helm\.sh/release-name}')" = "prometheus-operator-crds"
```

**Related**: W-001, W-002

### W-004: Missing planned test: step-4-dashboard-configmap-byte-identical-to-source (REQ-004)
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:276-340` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: Step 4 only asserts `/api/search?query=Keystone+Operator` returns ≥1 dashboard via Grafana. There is no sha256 or byte-equality assertion comparing the in-cluster ConfigMap content to the on-disk JSON at `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json`. The deploy-infra copy step is fragile — a future broken copy command would render an empty/old dashboard, and only this assertion would catch it.

**Code**:
```
step 4 (lines 276-340) only asserts Grafana /api/search?query=Keystone+Operator returns ≥1 dashboard
```

**Action Required**: Add a chainsaw assertion that fetches the keystone-operator-dashboard ConfigMap from monitoring, extracts the keystone-operator.json key, and compares its sha256 against operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json (the planned test specifies in-cluster sha256sum).

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a chainsaw script step that runs: SRC_HASH=$(sha256sum operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json | cut -d' ' -f1); CM_HASH=$(kubectl get configmap keystone-operator-dashboard -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.data.keystone-operator\.json}' | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1); test "$SRC_HASH" = "$CM_HASH"
```

---

### W-005: REQ-009 SKIP semantics use exit 0, not chainsaw skip
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:114-120` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: `exit 0` from a script step is reported by chainsaw as passed, not skipped (chainsaw has no step-level skip primitive). The spec literally says 'exits with SKIP (not FAIL)'. While default-safe and consistent with the existing tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/chainsaw-test.yaml convention, it deviates from the literal spec phrasing.

**Code**:
```
if ! kubectl get ns monitoring >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "SKIP: monitoring namespace not present."
  ...
  exit 0
fi
```

**Action Required**: Either accept the convention and update REQ-009's wording to match observed chainsaw semantics, or wire a chainsaw skip block conditioned on the absence of Namespace/monitoring if a future chainsaw version supports declarative skip.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Update REQ-009 spec wording to reflect chainsaw's pass-with-SKIP-echo semantics, since chainsaw has no step-level skip primitive. Alternatively, file an issue tracking adoption of declarative skip when chainsaw supports it.
```

**Related**: W-007

---

The implementation placed the kind-only posture write-up at docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md. Task 6.7 in the progress JSON is currently marked done despite the path mismatch.

```
### kube-prometheus-stack (kind-only opt-in, CC-0100) — placed at docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md:724-822 instead of architecture/docs/09-implementation/
```

**Action Required**: Either create the architecture/docs/09-implementation/ tree (add 11-kind-monitoring-stack.md with a short summary linking to the canonical doc), or update the spec/progress JSON to record that the canonical home for this kind-only posture is docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Update the spec/progress JSON for task 6.7 to record docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md as the canonical location, OR create architecture/docs/09-implementation/11-kind-monitoring-stack.md with a short summary linking to the canonical doc.
```

### W-007: REQ-009 Prometheus retry budget shape deviates from spec
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:225-256` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: Step 3 runs 8 attempts × (5s curl-timeout + 10s sleep) ≈ 120s, instead of the spec's 12 × 10s = 120s. Total elapsed time is comparable, but the iteration count differs from the planned test's '10s × 12 retry budget'. The header comment at lines 198-204 documents the trade-off explicitly. Defensible engineering call but deviates from the planned test wording.

**Code**:
```
step 3 polls 8 attempts × (5s curl-timeout + 10s sleep) ≈ 120s instead of 12 × 10s = 120s
```

**Action Required**: Document the deviation in the test header (already done) or update the planned test specification to reflect the actual implementation.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Update the REQ-009 planned test wording from '10s × 12 retry budget' to '8 attempts with 5s curl-timeout + 10s sleep ≈ 120s', or refactor step 3 to 12 × 10s polls if exact spec adherence is preferred.
```

**Related**: W-005, B-001

---

## INFO (3)

### I-001: nicolaka/netshoot:latest mutable tag in chainsaw probe pods
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml:232` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Aktualität neuer Dependencies

**Problem**: Four `kubectl run` invocations (lines 232, 265, 316, 348) use `--image=nicolaka/netshoot:latest`, a third-party image with a mutable tag. The chainsaw assertions (curl + jq output shape) are sensitive to upstream image changes, and a future netshoot retag could silently change the test surface. The repo elsewhere pins external action references by digest (e.g. actions/checkout@de0fac2e... # v6 in .github/workflows/ci.yaml:56) following the same review-pattern principle. Already noted in prior review as MINOR S3.

**Code**:
```
--image=nicolaka/netshoot:latest
```

**Action Required**: Pin the netshoot image to a tag + digest, or move to a small project-controlled probe image that ships curl + jq pre-installed. Replace all four occurrences (lines 232, 265, 316, 348).

**Suggested Fix**:
```
--image=docker.io/nicolaka/netshoot:v0.13@sha256:<digest>
```

---

### I-001: Positive deviation: e2e-prometheus also triggers on go_changed and any_e2e_tests
**File**: `hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh:114-123` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: ci-resolve-changes.sh emits e2e-prometheus=true when go_changed=true or any_e2e_tests=true, in addition to the explicit FILTER_e2e_prometheus. This means a Go-only operator change (no overlay/test/dashboard touch) still runs the prometheus lane. Mirrors the existing e2e-chaos pattern and provides broader coverage than the literal REQ-010 spec wording.

**Code**:
```
hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh:114-123 emits e2e-prometheus=true when go_changed=true or any_e2e_tests=true
```

**Action Required**: None — broader coverage is the correct posture for an opt-in lane that re-validates operator metrics. Note this in the spec/PR description.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Note in the PR description that e2e-prometheus also triggers on go_changed and any_e2e_tests for broader coverage, mirroring the e2e-chaos pattern. Update REQ-010 spec wording to reflect this if desired.
```

### I-002: Positive deviation: no-prometheus-when-disabled adds non-regression suite beyond spec
**File**: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/no-prometheus-when-disabled/chainsaw-test.yaml:1-137` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: feature-compliance

**Problem**: The new chainsaw-test.yaml positively asserts absence of Namespace/monitoring, HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack, and ServiceMonitor/keystone-operator on the default e2e-infra lane. The spec's planned test list mentioned this as default-cluster-has-no-monitoring-namespace but the implementation broadens it to also assert HelmRelease and ServiceMonitor absence. The e2e_infra path filter at .github/workflows/ci.yaml:92-96 already covers tests/e2e/infrastructure/**, so this runs on every PR that touches infra paths.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/infrastructure/no-prometheus-when-disabled/chainsaw-test.yaml (137 lines) asserts absence of Namespace/monitoring, HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack, and ServiceMonitor/keystone-operator
```

**Action Required**: None — closes a gap REQ-006 only implied.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Mention in the PR description that the implementation broadens the planned default-cluster-has-no-monitoring-namespace test to also cover HelmRelease and ServiceMonitor absence, closing a regression vector REQ-006 only implied.
```

---

## Summary

The PR ships a thorough kind-only opt-in for kube-prometheus-stack that mirrors the CC-0097 chaos-mesh precedent well: the overlay is self-contained, the production tree is byte-identical to main, the Grafana dashboard JSON remains the single source of truth, unit tests pin the gating logic, and the negative-case no-prometheus-when-disabled chainsaw suite is a strong default-state guard. The prior review's BLOCKING/CRITICAL items are all resolved. However, the e2e-prometheus job will fail at Step 2 because hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh ignores the spec.values patch on the suspended Flux HelmRelease, so the rendered chart never sets monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true. The remaining issues are documentation/consistency cleanups. (includes adversarial review pass)

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 1 |
| CRITICAL | 1 |
| WARNING  | 10 |
| INFO     | 3 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: NEEDS_CHANGES — Finding 1 (CRITICAL) must be fixed before merge: the e2e-prometheus chainsaw suite cannot pass without wiring WITH_PROMETHEUS through to ci-deploy-operator.sh (or reading the HelmRelease spec.values via --values). Findings 2-4 are merge-blocker-adjacent doc/consistency cleanups that should also be addressed; Finding 5 is optional polish.
resolution_summary: Addressed B-001 by threading WITH_PROMETHEUS through hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh (option (a) from the suggested fix): the script now reads WITH_PROMETHEUS as an optional env var, builds a helm_args array, and conditionally appends `--set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` when WITH_PROMETHEUS=true. The e2e-prometheus job's `Deploy operator` step in .github/workflows/ci.yaml was updated to export `WITH_PROMETHEUS: "true"` so the rendered chart now produces the ServiceMonitor in `openstack`, unblocking chainsaw steps 2 and 3. Additional changes outside the locations called out in B-001 were also made because the same review run produced WARNING/CRITICAL items beyond the single BLOCKING comment quoted here: hack/deploy-infra.sh (dropped the stale `Reviewer: please verify` comment per W-001), Makefile (added `stage-prometheus-dashboard` helper per W-002), docs/reference/ci-cd/ci-workflow.md and docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md (updated job count and added per-job sections for e2e-prometheus and shell-unit-tests-cc-0100 per W-003), and tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml (added the missing planned assertions: HelmRelease values rendered, no Alertmanager/node-exporter/kube-state-metrics workloads, no CRD ownership flap, dashboard ConfigMap byte-identical to source — addressing the WARNING-block W-001..W-004 items).
